name: The Fairmount State Bank
description_short: The Fairmount State Bank is a locally-owned community bank established in 1902, serving Fairmount, Indiana and surrounding areas with traditional banking services.
description_long:
The Fairmount State Bank was founded on December 18, 1902, and has operated as an independent, locally-owned community bank for over 120 years. The bank is physically located at 101 North Main Street in Fairmount, Indiana 46928, and has deep historical roots in the community, having relocated to its current corner location in 1911 and undergone renovations in 1965 and 1986 to modernize its facilities. As a member of The Federal Reserve System and the Better Business Bureau, the bank maintains regulatory standing and professional accountability standards.

The Fairmount State Bank offers essential retail banking services including checking and savings accounts, online banking access, account transfers between customer accounts, account balance inquiries, and transaction history viewing. Customers can access services through the main branch during business hours, online banking available 24 hours a day, and an ATM located inside the Village Pantry at the corner of Main Street and State Road 26. The bank emphasizes personal service alongside digital convenience, encouraging customers to enroll in online banking through the main branch.

What distinguishes this bank is its explicit commitment to community banking and local ownership. The website emphasizes that doing business with them means working with local people familiar with the local economy, differentiating them from national chains. The bank maintains a philosophy of balancing modern online banking conveniences with in-person relationship banking, stating they "still want to see you in person" and value the "personal touch that can only be done face to face." This positioning appeals to customers prioritizing local relationships over expansive service networks.

The Fairmount State Bank appears well-suited for customers seeking basic retail banking services in the Fairmount, Indiana area who value community relationships and local ownership. However, the website provides limited information about specific product offerings such as loan types, credit cards, investment services, or detailed account features. Customers requiring comprehensive financial services, competitive rates research, or services beyond basic checking/savings may need to contact the bank directly or consider larger regional institutions. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: ATM access through Village Pantry location
pros: Locally-owned and independent bank with 120+ years of community history since 1902
Member of The Federal Reserve System and Better Business Bureau for regulatory oversight
24/7 online banking available for account balance viewing and transfers between own accounts
ATM access inside Village Pantry for convenient cash withdrawals near Main Street
Emphasis on personal, face-to-face service alongside digital banking options
Familiar with local economy and community-specific financial needs
Simple online banking enrollment process available at main branch
cons: Limited service area restricted to Fairmount, Indiana and immediate surroundings
Website provides minimal detail about specific loan products, rates, or account features
No information about credit cards, investment services, or wealth management offerings
Single physical branch location limits accessibility for non-local customers
Website lacks pricing transparency and competitive rate information
best_for: Fairmount, Indiana residents seeking straightforward checking and savings accounts
Community-oriented customers prioritizing local ownership and personal banking relationships
Small business owners familiar with local bank staff and community networks
Customers valuing traditional banking convenience over extensive product selection
diagnosis: The Fairmount State Bank is best for Fairmount-area residents and small business owners seeking basic retail banking services from a locally-owned institution with personal relationships. The main caveat is the severely limited website information about product offerings, rates, and services—customers must contact the bank directly to evaluate specific account features and loan products for their needs.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
